column7: Typo – Please note that this is a suggested "Print Change". Merriam-Webster Dictionary, prefers this to be written as a Single Word. It is a bit like the word: "catfish" , which is NOT written "cat fish" !

Typo – Please note that this is a worthwhile GLOBAL "Print Change".  In Fact PLEASE Consider targeting the extracted element: "shṭh" —> "ṣṭh".  Of course, this is VERY similar in nature to your recent: "ñch" —> "ñc" (or some such combination).

Typo – Please note that this is a Headword Print Change. The neuter definitions are alreay given subsequently. The Main Headword is inteneded as Adjective. Thus again, we have the Global Correction relating to the abbreviation "a." in italics, which indicates "adjective". The Global Correction to be made here being: "m a." —> "m a".

Typo – UPDATED CORRECTION! Please note that this Correction is according to MW and VCP. The PROBLEM with AP listing Feminine Forms such as THIS, is that it REQUIRES interpretation !   LB ALSO, This is REALLY TOO MUCH: "kāyaka (-yikā f.), kāyika (-kī f.) + .-kā ".  THAT equation, should be reduced in some way !  For Example, simply: "kāyaka + (-yikā f.)", or you choose.
